title: Piquant Bitter Dutch Millet Stew
description: A slow cooked spicy Dutch millet stew with a bitter citrus twist, perfect for a romantic date night.
instructions: Rinse the millet under cold water until the water runs clear.
Heat the olive oil in a Dutch oven over medium heat and sauté the diced onion and minced garlic until translucent.
Add the diced carrot and celery to the pot and cook for another 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Stir in the smoked paprika, cayenne pepper, and chopped thyme, letting the spices bloom for 1 minute.
Add the rinsed millet to the pot, tossing to coat it in the aromatic mixture.
Pour in the bone broth, stirring to combine all ingredients, then bring to a gentle simmer.
Cover the pot, reduce the heat to low, and slow cook the stew for 150 minutes, stirring occasionally.
After 150 minutes, stir in the lemon zest, sea salt, and black pepper, adjusting seasoning to taste.
Fold in the chopped almonds and fresh spinach, allowing the spinach to wilt for 2 minutes.
Remove the pot from the heat, let the stew rest for 5 minutes, then serve hot.
